cards.back: SIMD stands for Single Instruction, Multiple Data. It is a type of parallel processing where a single instruction is applied to multiple data elements simultaneously.
MIMD stands for Multiple Instruction, Multiple Data. It is a type of parallel processing where multiple instructions are executed simultaneously on multiple data elements.

cards.front: What is the difference between Shared-memory and Distributed-memory?
cards.back: Shared-memory is a type of parallel memory where all processors share the same memory space, while Distributed-memory is a type of parallel memory where each processor has its own memory space.
Cache coherence is a mechanism used to ensure that data is consistent across multiple processors in a shared-memory system by synchronizing updates to the cache.
Interconnection networks are used to connect processors, memory units, and other components in a parallel computer system, enabling data transfer and communication between them.
